qa_pairs.question: What are the main distinctions users should be aware of regarding filesystems and software when transitioning between different general-purpose clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: While globally installed modules are consistent across all general-purpose clusters, user-installed Python virtual environments, R/Perl packages, or custom programs must be re-installed on each new cluster. Regarding filesystems, there is no mirroring of data between clusters; your home, scratch, and project spaces are independent, and any special storage allocations or increased project space quotas are specific to the cluster where they were made.

qa_pairs.question: What is the recommended method for transferring data between different clusters, especially for larger amounts?
qa_pairs.answer: To transfer data from one cluster to another, it is recommended to use Globus, particularly when the amount of data involved exceeds a few hundred megabytes.

qa_pairs.question: When submitting jobs on different clusters, what specific considerations should users keep in mind regarding job scripts and compute allocations?
qa_pairs.answer: While all clusters use Slurm, users should note that the number of CPU cores per node or GPU and the amount of memory per node or core can vary significantly, requiring job script adaptation. Furthermore, special compute allocations (like RRG or contributed allocations) are tied to specific clusters and are not available on others, although each research group has access to a default allocation on every cluster.
